questions.question_text: Which statement best describes pressure in pressure, winds, storms, and cyclones?

options.text: For a given force, pressure increases when the contact area decreases.
Pressure describes how force is distributed over an area.
Liquid pressure acts in all directions and generally increases with depth.
Atmospheric pressure is the pressure exerted by the weight of the air around Earth.

questions.explanation: Pressure describes how force is distributed over an area.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 6; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which example is most directly associated with pressure?

options.text: Water escapes farther from a lower hole in a tank than from a higher hole.
Snow shoes spread a person's weight over a larger area.
The same force produces more pressure on a sharp tip than on a broad end.
A suction cup holds when outside air pressure and the reduced pressure inside are different.

questions.explanation: The same force produces more pressure on a sharp tip than on a broad end.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 6; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which statement best distinguishes pressure from a nearby idea?

options.text: Air can exert pressure even though it is not visible.
Changing area changes pressure even if the total force remains unchanged.
Liquid pressure at a depth is not explained only by the amount of water visible above the container.
Pressure depends on force and area, not on force alone.

questions.explanation: Pressure depends on force and area, not on force alone.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 6; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which situation best applies the idea of pressure?

options.text: Increase area when a design should reduce pressure on a surface.
Use broad foundations or straps to reduce pressure on the supporting surface.
Compare holes at different depths while keeping the liquid and container arrangement controlled.
Use a carefully controlled demonstration to show that air pressure can support or move objects.

questions.explanation: Increase area when a design should reduce pressure on a surface.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 6; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

questions.question_text: Which statement corrects a common misunderstanding about pressure?

options.text: A broad base does not make the force disappear; it spreads it.
A larger contact area does not increase pressure when the force stays the same.
Liquid pressure does not act only downward.
Air pressure is not absent simply because air cannot be seen.

questions.explanation: A larger contact area does not increase pressure when the force stays the same.
questions.source_locator: Chapter 6; original question derived from the chapter concept manifest

options.text: Liquid pressure acts in all directions and generally increases with depth.

questions.question_text: Which statement best describes storms in pressure, winds, storms, and cyclones?

options.text: A cyclone is a large rotating weather system with strong winds around a low-pressure region.

questions.question_text: Which statement best distinguishes storms from a nearby idea?
